note: P-panel (M5, parity-checklist §G) — the panel becomes a real dock: resizable, collapsible, with tabs anchored at the 'reveal' strip so migrated affordances have a home (the §B prerequisite). Today the aside is fixed at 330px, permanently open, and its body (reveal layers · browse tree · inspector) is at content capacity — no migrated feature can land in it. The fix: (1) a PURE dock-state reducer ufDockReduce (unfold-esc/unfold-lift precedent: acceptance-bearing logic in its own dependency-free file inside the unfold module) owning every dock decision — tab switching, collapse, width clamping, and persisted-state normalization — so the chrome behaviour is red-then-green provable, not screenshot prose; (2) the panel DOM consumes it: the strip literally typed 'reveal' at the top of the panel becomes the tab row (reveal · io · mermaid), a chevron collapses the panel to a slim rail, a drag handle on the panel's left border resizes it, and the dock state persists across reloads; (3) BATCHED §B migrations that the tabs exist to host, landing in the same pass: an io tab (save .mmd · load .mmd · load bodies.json — files module code paths, exposed on FilesApi instead of duplicated) and a mermaid tab (serialised text · apply · copy — the mermaid module stays the only serialiser). Assumptions recorded (amend later if wrong): (1) dock state persists in localStorage under 'unfold.dock' as a GLOBAL chrome preference — deliberately not the per-diagram ViewSpec, which owns what you look at, not how the chrome is arranged; (2) width clamps to [240, 580], default 330 (current width); (3) selecting any tab always expands a collapsed panel (a tab click must reveal its body); clicking the active tab is a no-op — collapse is only the chevron's job; (4) resize while collapsed is a no-op (the handle is hidden with the panel); (5) the collapsed rail keeps only the expand chevron — sleek/clean is the ruled hard constraint; (6) the mermaid tab applies text through ctx.dom.mmd + mermaid.applyText() so the mermaid module remains the single parse/apply path (no second textarea-independent apply); (7) nav/slice/style §B rows stay unmigrated — nav overlaps unfold's existing browse search (needs a design ruling, not a port), slice and style are design-heavy; they land as future tabs on this infrastructure. Resume: npm run flowmap:status -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/m5-p-panel.plan.json · prove behaviour: npm run flowmap:acceptance -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/m5-p-panel.plan.json · runtime criteria (browser, 0-context verify): (1) the panel top shows the tab row reveal · io · mermaid where the 'reveal' text was; (2) the reveal tab shows today's body unchanged (layers, browse, inspector); (3) the io tab offers save .mmd (downloads), load .mmd (round-trips a saved file), load bodies.json (source pane fills); (4) the mermaid tab shows the current serialised text, apply round-trips an edit, copy fills the clipboard; (5) dragging the panel's left border resizes it within clamps; the chevron collapses to a slim rail and back; (6) reload restores tab, width and collapsed state; (7) zero console errors through boot → tab switching → resize → collapse → reload.

phases.title: Pure dock decision
phases.subtitle: ufDockReduce: tab switching, collapse, width clamping, persisted-state normalization — acceptance-tested red before code

phases.title: Dock shell
phases.subtitle: the reveal strip becomes the tab row; collapse chevron + rail; left-border resize handle; state persisted and restored

phases.title: Batched §B tabs
phases.subtitle: io tab (save/load .mmd, load bodies.json via FilesApi) and mermaid tab (text/apply/copy via the one serialiser)

intent.problem: The panel has no state model at all: it is a fixed-width always-open aside whose sections are hard-stacked in the DOM. 'Which body shows, how wide, collapsed or not' does not exist as a decision anywhere — so tabs, resize and collapse would otherwise land as unprovable DOM-bound logic.
intent.approach: One pure reducer ufDockReduce(state, action, tabs) → DockState with DockState = { tab, collapsed, width }. Rules: (a) setTab to a known tab selects it AND expands a collapsed panel (a tab click always reveals its body); (b) setTab to an unknown tab or the already-active tab returns the state unchanged; (c) toggleCollapse flips collapsed, preserving tab and width; (d) resize rounds and clamps width to [240, 580] and is a no-op while collapsed; (e) load normalizes a persisted raw value — non-object input, an unknown stored tab, a non-boolean collapsed or a non-finite width each fall back to the defaults (first tab, expanded, 330), a valid stored width is clamped; (f) an empty tabs list makes every action a no-op (boot guard). Pure, deterministic, dependency-free file src/panel/unfold-dock.ts.
intent.rationale: E2/H1 factor-to-pure in practice (ufEscAction/ufLiftWires precedent, same multi-file-module placement): the load-bearing chrome claims — a tab click always reveals, width never escapes its clamps, a corrupt persisted value can never wedge the panel shut — become red-then-green acceptance cases instead of prose.

fm.name: ufDockReduce
fm.description: pure dock-state reducer for the primary surface's panel: DockState { tab, collapsed, width } advances only through actions — setTab selects a known tab and always expands a collapsed panel (unknown or already-active tabs are no-ops), toggleCollapse flips collapsed preserving tab and width, resize rounds and clamps width to [240, 580] and is a no-op while collapsed, load normalizes a persisted raw value falling back per-field to first-tab / expanded / 330; an empty tabs list makes every action a no-op

cases.name: setTab to a known tab switches the body
setTab to an unknown tab is a no-op
setTab while collapsed also expands — a tab click always reveals its body
setTab to the already-active tab while expanded is a no-op (collapse belongs to the chevron)
toggleCollapse collapses, preserving tab and width
resize rounds a fractional drag width
resize while collapsed is a no-op (the handle is hidden with the panel)
load of a non-object persisted value falls back to the defaults
load restores a valid persisted state verbatim
load normalizes per-field: unknown tab, non-boolean collapsed and non-finite width each fall back
load clamps a stored width that escaped the bounds
an empty tabs list makes every action a no-op (boot guard)

intent.problem: The aside is a fixed 330px always-open stack: the strip literally typed 'reveal' is a dead section header, there is no way to reclaim the space, and no migrated affordance has anywhere to land.
intent.approach: The panel consumes the pure reducer: the reveal strip becomes the tab row (reveal · io · mermaid, styled on the existing .uf-sech grammar with a collapse chevron at its right edge); the three existing sections (layers, browse, inspector) become the reveal tab's body, untouched; io and mermaid get body containers of their own; a drag handle on the panel's left border resizes through the reducer; collapsing leaves a slim rail holding only the expand chevron; every reduced state paints via one applyDock() and persists to localStorage 'unfold.dock' (global chrome preference, deliberately not the per-diagram ViewSpec); expand/collapse/resize end with the existing reframe so the stage reclaims the space. deps widen to { selection, camera, files, mermaid } — main.ts (the composition root) already builds files and mermaid before unfold.
intent.rationale: The chrome decision lives in the proven reducer; the DOM is a dumb painter of it (deriveView seam direction). Anchoring the tabs at the literal 'reveal' strip is Chris's ruling verbatim, and the §B migrations stop being homeless.

fm.name: initUnfold
fm.description: build the primary surface (own DOM + own CSS, the planner isolation pattern) bound to ctx; reads ctx.state + ctx.bodies, writes only through the shared hooks path; the dock carries a temporary legacy-compare affordance instead of a ✕ (M4 correction); the panel is a real dock driven by the pure ufDockReduce — tabs (reveal · io · mermaid) anchored at the reveal strip, chevron collapse to a slim rail, left-border drag resize, state persisted under unfold.dock

interfaces.name: initUnfold
accepts: deps: { selection: SelectionApi; camera: CameraApi; files: FilesApi; mermaid: MermaidApi }

intent.problem: The load paths are welded to legacy DOM: loadInput/bodiesInput onchange handlers own the only route into 'apply this .mmd text' and 'apply this bodies.json object'. The unfold io tab cannot reach them without duplicating parse/apply logic — the exact drift flowmap exists to kill.
intent.approach: Factor the two handler bodies into named functions and expose them on FilesApi: loadMmdText(text) does exactly what the legacy input did (mmd.value = text → mermaid.applyText() → camera.zoomToFit()) and loadBodies(raw) is the existing applyBodies. The legacy hidden inputs keep working by calling the same functions. saveMmd is already exposed.
intent.rationale: One code path per verb regardless of surface — the io tab and the legacy inputs are two triggers of the same proven behaviour, so the §B rows migrate without a second implementation.

fm.name: initFiles
fm.description: wire save plus the hidden .mmd and bodies.json file inputs, bound to ctx; the load verbs are exposed on FilesApi (loadMmdText, loadBodies) so the unfold io tab and the legacy inputs share one code path

intent.problem: FilesApi's public surface is saveMmd alone — the io tab's other two verbs have no typed door.
intent.approach: Widen the interface: saveMmd() stays; loadMmdText(text: string): void and loadBodies(raw: unknown): void join it.
intent.rationale: The composition root passes files into unfold's deps; the type is the contract the map gates.
